learningObjective: Count the perimeter of shapes on a grid
coreIdea: Perimeter is the total distance around a shape, counted as the number of unit edges.
examples: A square that is 2 units wide has a perimeter of 8 units. 🔲
A rectangle that is 3 units by 2 units has a perimeter of 10 units. 🔲
A shape that covers 4 unit squares on the edge has a perimeter of 4 units. 🔲
misconceptions: Students may confuse perimeter with area when counting on a grid.
Students may count the inside squares instead of the edges for perimeter.
Students may forget to count all four sides when finding the perimeter.

hints: Count the edges around the shape.
Each side of a unit square is one edge.
Add up all the edges to find the perimeter.
